description_long: This is a tall, cylindrical stone tower with a single, ornate roof structure at the top, reminiscent of East Asian pagodas. The roof is covered in textured, orange tiles and features four prominent, upward-curving eaves at the corners, each with decorative detailing. The main entrance is an arched opening just below the roof, with a small balcony or platform extending outward. The stone body of the structure is irregularly shaped, with protruding stonework and a weathered, ancient appearance. The base is slightly wider and reinforced, while the upper section is adorned with wooden or tiled elements. At the apex of the roof sits a pointed, ornamental finial, adding to the traditional aesthetic.
description: A tall, cylindrical stone pagoda with an ornate, orange-tiled roof, upward-curving eaves, and a decorative finial at the top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched entrance, balcony, and the detailed roof with upward-curving eaves.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stonework, the full circular balcony, and the symmetry of the roof eaves.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back stone wall, roof eaves, and a small protruding section at the base.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the entrance from the side, the balcony, and the ornate roof structure.
